mission.title: Install and Configure OpenKruise on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenKruise is a CNCF incubating project that extends Kubernetes with advanced workload controllers including CloneSet, Advanced StatefulSet, SidecarSet, BroadcastJob, Advanced DaemonSet, and more. It automates the management of large-scale applications with enhanced capabilities beyond native Kubernetes controllers.

steps.title: Add the OpenKruise Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OpenKruise Helm chart repository:
```bash
helm repo add openkruise https://openkruise.github.io/charts/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenKruise using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenKruise in the 'kruise-system' namespace:
```bash
helm install kruise openkruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system --create-namespace --version 1.8.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenKruise controller manager and daemon pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kruise-system
```

You should see `kruise-controller-manager` and `kruise-daemon` pods in Running state.

resolution.summary: A successful installation shows kruise-controller-manager and kruise-daemon pods running in the 'kruise-system' namespace. You can then use OpenKruise CRDs like CloneSet, SidecarSet, and BroadcastJob to manage workloads.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add openkruise https://openkruise.github.io/charts/
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall OpenKruise:
```bash
helm uninstall kruise --namespace kruise-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove all OpenKruise Custom Resource Definitions:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kruise.io | awk '{print $1}' | xargs kubectl delete crd
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kruise-system' namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kruise-system
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest chart version:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenKruise
upgrade.description:
Upgrade to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kruise openkruise/kruise --namespace kruise-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check pod status after upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kruise-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
Check for insufficient resources:
```bash
kubectl describe pod -l control-plane=controller-manager -n kruise-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the controller manager logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l control-plane=controller-manager -n kruise-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Webhook failures
troubleshooting.description:
If workload creation fails with webhook errors, check the kruise-webhook-service:
```bash
kubectl get svc kruise-webhook-service -n kruise-system
kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep kruise
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster (v1.22+) with Helm and kubectl installed.
